methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 10 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $500M - $9.2B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Hidden Level
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Kodiak Robotics is a Series C-stage defense and security company that has raised $250M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 same-sector peers at equivalent or adjacent stages, comprising 8 private companies and 2 public names (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS). Key private comparables include Saronic ($9.2B, Series D), Chaos Industries ($4.5B, Series D), and Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, and Harmattan AI (each $1.4–1.5B, Series B).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using $100M as a proxy for total capital deployed, applies a 1.7x multiplier typical for Series C advancement, yielding $170M. Peer-median analysis across the 10-company cohort produces $1.4B, with a range spanning $500M to $9.2B. This wide dispersion reflects maturity variance within the defense tech segment—Series D companies command material premiums over earlier stages.

The triangulated valuation range spans $144M (low) to $1.6B (high), with a median of $772M. The stage-progression method anchors the lower bound, while peer comparables establish the upper bound. Key uncertainty centers on Kodiak's relative technical capability and customer traction versus the peer set, which materially influences placement within the $500M–$1.4B band most defensible for Series C stage.
